commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; maintainers should decide whether the supplied dispatcher/Crabbox proof is enough or whether to request live QQBot proof before landing.; Cleared: The diff is limited to QQBot dispatch control flow, tests, changelog, and one test assertion; it adds no dependencies, permissions, secret handling, scripts, or new network destinations. (sha=a174f9afe2f9717ee2f1a9c4921890319192197d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates QQBot outbound dispatch to send text-only tool progress immediately when QQBot streaming is enabled, adds regression coverage and a changelog entry, and includes a small OpenAI video test assertion hardening.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main buffers text-only kind tool payloads and clears the fallback timer when a later block arrives, so progress text is not delivered before the final response. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; maintainers should decide whether the supplied dispatcher/Crabbox proof is enough or whether to request live QQBot proof before landing.

target.title: fix(qqbot): deliver partial tool progress
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #66509.
- QQBot now delivers text-only `kind: "tool"` progress immediately when streaming progress is enabled.
- Keeps streaming-off behavior buffered/final-only and leaves media, voice, final block, and official C2C stream-controller ownership unchanged.

## Root Cause
`extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts` buffered text-only tool deliveries in `toolTexts` and started a fallback timer. When a later block reply arrived, the final block path cleared that timer and delivered the final answer without flushing the buffered tool text, so users saw silence until the final response even with partial streaming enabled.

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: QQBot text-only `kind: "tool"` progress no longer disappears when a later `kind: "block"` response arrives under legacy partial streaming or recommended `streaming: true` C2C streaming.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout and AWS Crabbox Linux validation for the QQBot outbound dispatcher path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox fresh-pr run `run_275af13012e5` on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_d5b568493e7c`: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose; CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 pnpm check:changed`

Evidence after fix:
```text
streaming true:
sendText -> Working: checking logs
sendText -> final answer

partial streaming object:
sendText -> Working: checking logs
sendText -> final answer

streaming off:
sendText -> final answer
```

Observed result after fix: recommended `streaming: true` and legacy `{ mode: "partial" }` send text-only tool progress before the final answer; `streaming: false` preserves final-only behavior. AWS Crabbox also passed the QQBot regression test and `pnpm check:changed`.

What was not tested: live QQBot network transport.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities: No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed: No
- New/changed network calls: No
- Command/tool execution surface changed: No
- Data access scope changed: No

## Scope Boundary
Only QQBot outbound dispatch changes. Shared reply semantics, media fallback, final block delivery, and official C2C streaming controller paths stay unchanged.
target.author: samzong

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
